reasoning: The informal statement claims that every group of order 200 has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup. The Lean signature assumes `G` is a finite group with `card G = 200` and concludes `∃ N : Sylow 5 G, N.Normal`, which says there exists a Sylow 5-subgroup of G that is normal. The type `Sylow 5 G` in Mathlib is exactly the type of Sylow 5-subgroups of G, and `N.Normal` is the standard notion of being a normal subgroup. The quantifier structure (existence of a normal Sylow 5-subgroup) matches the informal claim precisely. No extra hypotheses are added beyond what is implicitly required (finiteness via Fintype, which is needed to even talk about a group of order 200).
